commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=9281e2535a484d65cefac9a3a685a7bdfa193743)
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates CI to centralize the Swift version, restore an Actions cache for `~/.local/share/swiftly`, and run the Linux CLI matrix through that cached swiftly state.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection rather than a completed live warm-cache run: restore the swiftly home cache so `config.installedToolchains` contains Swift 6.2.1, then `swiftly install` returns before emitting the prerequisite script.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor warm-cache proof and maintainer review of the cache-hit prerequisite repair before merge.

target.title: ci: cache Linux Swift toolchains
target.body:
## Summary
- cache the Linux swiftly home/toolchain state used by the `build-linux-cli` matrix
- centralize the Swift version in workflow `env.SWIFT_VERSION`
- keep the swiftly bootstrap download/init on every run, so CI never executes a cached `swiftly` bootstrap binary
- print the Actions cache-hit value in the Swift install step for easier CI diagnosis

## Why
Every PR runs the Linux x64 and arm64 CLI jobs, and each job currently installs Swift 6.2.1 through swiftly before building/testing the CLI. Caching `~/.local/share/swiftly` lets warm runs reuse Swift toolchain state while keeping the existing `swiftly install ... --use` command as the correctness gate.

This does not change test coverage or branch-protection behavior. Cold runs still follow the existing download/init/install path. Warm runs still fetch the official swiftly bootstrap, then let swiftly reuse cached toolchain state.

## Validation
- workflow YAML parsed with `yaml.safe_load`
- checked the `build-linux-cli` job contains `actions/cache@v4` with an OS/arch/Swift-version scoped key
- checked the cache path is limited to `~/.local/share/swiftly`
- extracted the embedded Swift install shell step and ran `bash -n`
- `git diff --check`

## GitHub Actions proof
Run for this commit: https://github.com/steipete/CodexBar/actions/runs/27857529840

- `changes`: success
- `lint`: success
- `build-linux-cli (linux-x64, ubuntu-24.04)`: success
  - `Restore Swift toolchain cache`: success
  - log: `Cache not found for input keys: swift-Linux-X64-6.2.1-v2`
  - log: `Swift toolchain cache hit:` (blank on expected first cold run)
  - log: `Swift version 6.2.1 (swift-6.2.1-RELEASE)`
  - release build, Linux tests, and CLI smoke test: success
- `build-linux-cli (linux-arm64, ubuntu-24.04-arm)`: success
  - `Restore Swift toolchain cache`: success
  - log: `Cache not found for input keys: swift-Linux-ARM64-6.2.1-v2`
  - log: `Swift toolchain cache hit:` (blank on expected first cold run)
  - log: `Swift version 6.2.1 (swift-6.2.1-RELEASE)`
  - release build, Linux tests, and CLI smoke test: success

The first v2 run is expected to be cold because the cache key changed to remove the cached-bootstrap boundary. The completed Linux matrix proves the cache restore/save hooks and conservative install path do not break either architecture. Subsequent runs with the same OS/arch/Swift-version key should report `Swift toolchain cache hit: true` while still downloading the official swiftly bootstrap before `swiftly install`.
target.author: yuxin-qiao

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
